THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 26, 2009 ON THE COURT'S MOTION. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FRANDO CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STAY COLONY ACTION (PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION GRANTED. THE EXISTENCE OF A WALL BETWEEN THE DEMOLISHED BUILDING AND THE ADJACENT BUILDING IS A FACTUAL QUESTION RELEVANT TO THE LIABILITY ACTION AND THE COVERAGE ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Colony Action
Off Calendar Oct-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FRANDO CORPORATION'S Motion To Stay Colony Action (plaintiff's complaint).
APPEARANCES: Raymond Coates, Esq. of Low, Ball & Lynch 981-6630 for pltf & opposing party; Gary R. Selvin, Esq. of Selvin Wraith Halman LLP 510/874-1811 for deft & moving party (appearing via CourtCall).
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion granted. The existence of a wall between the demolished building and the adjacent building is a factual question relevant to the liability action and the coverage action. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
